Remove duplicated hook definitions from SKILL.md frontmatter (#3)

* Remove duplicated hook definitions from SKILL.md frontmatter

Hooks are already defined in .claude-plugin/hooks/hooks.json, which
provides always-on enforcement after plugin installation. The SKILL.md
frontmatter duplicated these definitions, creating a maintenance burden.
Keep hooks.json as the single source of truth for lifecycle hooks, and
SKILL.md for the name, description, and body of guidance.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01SRiEma4LEwuN8jkaHF7uUz

* Add audit skill as a manual /audit slash command

Pure slash command — no hooks, no automatic triggers. The user invokes
/audit when they want a holistic pre-push review of the full branch delta.
Covers: unintended changes, secrets, debug artifacts, test coverage, build
verification, commit hygiene, integration wiring, and merge conflicts.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01SRiEma4LEwuN8jkaHF7uUz

* Remove audit skill — moving to standalone repo

The audit skill doesn't use any hooks and shouldn't be coupled to the
guardrails plugin. Extracting it to its own repo so it can be installed
independently.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01SRiEma4LEwuN8jkaHF7uUz

---------

commit.message:
Remove bucket clamping — let ensemble drive bucket picks directly

Clamping snapped the bucket to AccuWeather's latest value, causing
flip-flopping as AccuWeather revised intraday (47→48→49→50). The
ensemble (50.6°F) had the right bucket the whole time while the
clamp forced "49° or below" — which was wrong (actual: 50°F).

Now the ensemble drives the bucket and probabilities give users
honest confidence info + runner-up for hedging decisions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add ML stacker meta-learner for probability estimation

GradientBoosted tree (HistGradientBoosting from sklearn) that learns
non-linear interactions between sources from resolved bets.

- Cold start: <100 bets = disabled, 100-300 = light model, 300+ = full
- Features: ensemble/historical/det/analog/bma probs, horizon, confidence
- Safety: only activates if CV Brier beats current linear blending
- Blends at 35% ML / 65% linear weight, re-normalized
- Trains weekly (Sunday 11:30 UTC) via scheduler
- API: GET /ai/ml-stacker/status, POST /ai/ml-stacker/train
- Model persisted as pickle blob in DuckDB with 1h TTL cache

commit.message:
fix: stale book inventory reduction + exit duplicate sell prevention

Two bugs from VPS production logs:

1. Stale book blocked ALL quoting when holding inventory — bot sat
   26min with 5 DOWN shares doing nothing. Now places SELL at cached
   best_bid+tick (maker) to reduce risk even with stale book data.

2. EXIT regime spammed duplicate sell orders every tick while waiting
   for fill — caused 8x "not enough balance" errors. Now checks for
   pending SELL orders before generating new exit intents.

feat: add GE_REQUIREMENTS cross-reference for accurate course completion detection

- scrape_ge_requirements.py: one-time scraper that fetches catalog.byu.edu
  GE subpages and merges with pathways.py course pools; outputs 203 course
  codes across 13 categories to ge_requirements.py
- ge_requirements.py: auto-generated authoritative map of GE category →
  valid course codes (used as import by app and optimizer)
- app.py: import GE_REQUIREMENTS; add _completed_from_courses() helper that
  cross-references taken course codes against GE_REQUIREMENTS; apply in both
  PDF and MyMap flows so completion is detected by course code, not just text
  pattern matching
- optimizer.py: import GE_REQUIREMENTS; use it in _resolve_requirements() to
  subtract GE_REQUIREMENTS-confirmed completions from remaining categories,
  supplementing PathwaySolver results

Add V12.41 vs best comparison notebook, fix timing instrumentation, and holdout search updates

- New notebook: notebooks/v1241_vs_best_comparison.ipynb — compares V12.41 (86.5% AR) vs
  current best (15% AR) across Z-space quality, encoder heads, holdout search, AR decoding,
  neighborhood analysis, and decoder attractors
- Fix TimingStats: auxiliary losses (type, numden, stop, site_dup, HP, SC, family, physics_z,
  hungarian, doping) were untracked between decoder_fwd stop and loss_compute start — 58% of
  epoch time was invisible. Moved loss_compute start to cover full loss block.
- Fix format_summary() mixed denominator bug: tracked phases used sum-of-tracked as base while
  "Other" used epoch wall-clock, causing >100% totals. All now use wall-clock.
- Add targeted MP non-SC ingestion (scripts/ingest_mp_targeted.py) for rare element neighborhoods
- Add holdout Z pool preparation (scripts/analysis/prepare_holdout_z_pool.py) for Phase 2 Strategy 5
- Add exploration-guided sample weights, RL AR gate, holdout search improvements
- Update training config for V12.41 compatibility (vocab 148, digit-by-digit fractions)

commit.message:
Harden mission lifecycle: 7 safety fixes for production trading

1. Vault key creation now fails mission (was soft-fail, left missions
   without Master EOA → activation would fail with confusing error)
2. Deposit checker idempotency: re-activating a DEPOSITING mission
   returns early instead of enqueuing duplicate deposit checks
3. LLM filter wrapped in try/except per signal — one filter failure
   no longer kills all remaining signals for that mission
4. Intra-cycle margin tracking — available margin decremented as
   positions open within same cycle (was using stale withdrawable)
5. Allowed assets validated against platform list at creation time
6. Circuit breaker now records per-trade failures (was only tracking
   total job exceptions, missing individual order failures)
7. Intra-cycle dedup: tracks assets_opened_this_cycle to prevent
   duplicate positions when same signal appears twice

commit.message:
feat: 3c export targets — Qiskit, Cirq, Q#, pyQuil

circuit.toQiskit()  — Python QuantumCircuit API; covers H/X/Y/Z/S/T,
  rx/ry/rz, u1/u2/u3, cx/cy/cz/ch, crx/cry/crz, cu1/cu3, ccx, cswap,
  rxx/ryy/rzz, iswap; cs/ct/csdg/ctdg → cu1(±π/n).

circuit.toCirq()    — Google Cirq; LineQubit + cirq.Circuit([...]) style;
  ZPowGate for s†/t†, .controlled() for cy/ch/crx/cry/crz/cu1, ccx/cswap.

circuit.toQSharp()  — Microsoft Q# namespace with ResetAll; Adjoint S/T,
  Controlled Rx/Ry/Rz, Controlled S/T and their adjoints, ccnot/cswap.

circuit.toPyQuil()  — Rigetti pyQuil; dynamic gate import list, DAGGER
  combinator for S†/T†, iswap. Throws for controlled-rotation gates.

Shared fmtAngle(r, piToken) helper produces clean angle expressions
(pi/2, math.pi/4, PI()/8.0) across all backends.
iswap/srswap/xy now carry GateMeta for serialization routing.

39 new tests, 322 total, ~120ms.

commit.message:
Fix mobile tap-to-move by bypassing R3F event system

OrbitControls intercepts pointer events on touch devices, preventing
onPointerUp from firing on instancedMesh (board overlay). The fix uses
a MobileTapHandler component that listens to raw DOM touchend events on
the canvas, raycasts against the board plane (y=0), and converts the
world-space hit point directly to grid coordinates. Also added a 200ms
debounce to handleCellClick to prevent double-fires when both the touch
handler and piece pointer handlers fire for the same tap.

commit.message:
Replace silent fail-open with user-facing error messages

Critical agent failures (Question, Followup, Confirmation) now throw
AgentError instead of silently yielding raw question text. The chat
route catches these and sends a user-safe message via SSE:
"Something went wrong on our end. Please refresh the page and try
again."

Non-critical agents (Examples, Review Writer) still fail silently
since the main agent can proceed without them.

Changes:
- orchestrator: AgentError class, throw on critical failures, log
  all failures to execution_logs for the debug panel
- chat route: AgentError-aware SSE error handler, internal details
  only exposed in development mode
- frontend: updated exhausted-retries message to match

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: make avatar image persistence robust with retries at every layer

Root cause: kie-avatar-callback and generate-avatar-status both silently
fell back to temporary Kie.ai display URLs when Supabase Storage uploads
failed. These URLs expire, breaking saved avatars.

Fixes:
- kie-avatar-callback: retry persistToStorage 3x with backoff (1s, 2s)
- generate-avatar-status: retry persistToStorage 3x with backoff for
  newly polled images, plus a re-persist pass that catches any temp URLs
  left over from callback failures
- CreateAvatar.tsx: updated error message (no upload workaround)

The pipeline now has 3 layers of persistence defense:
1. Callback (immediate, 3 retries)
2. Status polling (re-persist any temp URLs, 3 retries)
3. Client save (persistAvatarImage, blocks save on failure)
